hyperparameter|||from paper context|in this work, as in [#], local updates in batches are counted rather than local epochs, aligning with theoretical formulations and extending to related strategies based on fedavg.|7|

hyperparameter|||from paper context|client updates are computed as: $$ \begin{array}{l} \delta_ {t} = \frac {#}{| s |} \sum_ {i \in s} \delta_ {i} ^ {t} \\ m _ {t} = \beta_ {#} m _ {t - #} + (# - \beta_ {#}) \delta_ {t} \tag {#} \\ v _ {t} = \beta_ {#} v _ {t - #} + (# - \beta_ {#}) (\delta_ {t}) ^ {#} \\ x _ {t + #} = x _ {t} - \eta \frac {m _ {t}}{\sqrt {v _ {t}} + \tau} \\ \end{array} $$ where $\delta _ { t }$ is the aggregated gradient update at time t, $s$ is the set of clients participating, $| s |$ is the number of clients in $s$ , $m _ { t }$ and $v _ { t }$ are the first and second moment estimates, $\beta _ { # }$ and $\beta _ { # }$ are the exponential decay rates for these moment estimates, $\eta$ is the learning rate, and $\tau$ is a small constant for numerical stability.|1|

hyperparameter|||from paper context|updates for the global and local control variables and model parameters are computed as follows: $$ \begin{array}{l} \boldsymbol {y} _ {i} \leftarrow \boldsymbol {y} _ {i} - \eta_ {l} \left(g _ {i} \left(\boldsymbol {y} _ {i}\right) + \boldsymbol {c} - \boldsymbol {c} _ {i}\right) \\ c _ {i} ^ {+} \leftarrow (\mathrm {i}) g _ {i} (\boldsymbol {x}), \text {o r (i i)} \boldsymbol {c} _ {i} - \boldsymbol {c} + \frac {#}{k \eta_ {l}} (\boldsymbol {x} - \boldsymbol {y} _ {i}) \\ \boldsymbol {x} \leftarrow \boldsymbol {x} + \frac {\eta_ {g}}{| s |} \sum_ {i \in s} (\boldsymbol {y} _ {i} - \boldsymbol {x}) \\ \boldsymbol {c} \gets \boldsymbol {c} + \frac {#}{n} \sum_ {i \in s} (\boldsymbol {c} _ {\boldsymbol {i}} ^ {+} - \boldsymbol {c} _ {\boldsymbol {i}}) \\ \end{array} $$ where $\mathbf { { \mathit { y } } } _ { i }$ is client update performed $k$ times, $\eta$ is the learning rate, $g _ { i } ( y _ { i } )$ is the mini-batch gradient, and $_ { \textbf { \em x } }$ are the server parameters.|1|
